title: USD Single-Layer Open Contract

included: open one resolved local USD layer resource
resource protocol I/O before concrete format handler invocation
extension-first format dispatch from ResolvedLocation metadata
resource-signature fallback dispatch for ambiguous resources without a registered extension match
generic dispatch to registered USDA and USDC layer-format handlers
generic dispatch to the registered USDZ package layer handler for .usdz local resources and Package resolved locations
file-backed fast-path forwarding to concrete handlers when present
return of parsed layer data as usd-document-model storage
canonical dump adapter from the stored single-layer data model
clear diagnostics for unsupported resources, unsupported formats, malformed USDA, USDC, or USDZ content, and duplicate spec paths
deferred: asset resolver search paths and resolver callbacks
sublayer, reference, payload, inherits, specializes, variant, relocates, and value clip composition

model.RootInput: A concrete resolved local resource location supplied to the target entrypoint.
model.ResourceOpen: The resource protocol step that turns the resolved location into opened resource bytes or a file-backed byte view.
model.LayerFormatDispatch: A generic format-dispatch boundary with registered USDA and USDC handlers in active target scopes.
model.LayerData: The document-model layer/spec/field storage populated by the selected layer-format parser.
model.LayerOpenResult: A target-native result containing either LayerData or diagnostics. It is not a JSON object.
model.CanonicalDump: The language-neutral ok, diagnostics, and layer JSON result used only by integration adapters and goldens.

input.resolvedLocation: resolved local file or supported local file URI

output.result: target-native LayerOpenResult containing a document-model Layer or diagnostics

input.openedResourceBytes: opened resource bytes or file-backed byte view from a successful ResourceReadResult

output.result: target-native LayerOpenResult containing a document-model Layer or unsupported-format diagnostic

laws: USDA opened-resource byte handling must consume usda-layer-open
USDC opened-resource byte handling must consume usdc-layer-open when that handler is registered in the active target scope
USDZ opened-resource byte handling must consume usdz-layer-open when that handler is registered in the active target scope
format dispatch must consult the ResolvedLocation display and extension before consulting opened resource signatures; suffix matching on the raw identifier before resolution is a contract violation

when a registered handler matches a resolved .usda or .usdc extension, the opener must select that handler without reading resource content to inspect a header or magic value for the dispatch decision

opened resource signatures are a fallback only for ambiguous resources with no registered extension match; mismatched opened bytes under an explicit .usda or .usdc extension are diagnosed by the selected concrete handler, not redispatched to another handler

when usd-resource-protocol exposes file_backed_path on the opened ResourceReadResult, the opener must forward that path to standalone USDA and USDC concrete handlers so format-specific fast paths can run

file_backed_path must not be forwarded to an inner USDC handler selected from a USDZ package unless it names the same byte extent; USDZ entries pass an entry byte view and leave standalone file_backed_path unset
parsed specs and fields must be stored through usd-document-model before returning a successful layer result
open_layer and dispatch_opened_resource must not return Json, dict, tagged JSON, or canonical dump trees as their domain result
JSON materialization is allowed only in explicit adapters, diagnostics serialization, and dump commands
the generic layer opener must not parse USDA grammar directly
the generic layer opener must not parse USDC binary data directly
the generic layer opener must not parse ZIP central directories or USDZ package entries directly
the generic layer opener must not implement composition, value resolution, stage population, or schemas
generated targets must pass their active scope goldens for this contract

adapter_protocol.input: The current integration adapter is the generated dump command. It receives a USDA or USDC filepath as its final argument.
